name: Grodno Region (Belarus)

colors.symbolism: the golden aurochs and mural crown

description: A red field bearing a golden aurochs (European bison) beneath a golden mural crown, from the arms of Grodno Region.
history: Grodno Region (Hrodna Voblast) is the north-western region of Belarus, bordering Poland and Lithuania and centred on Grodno. Its flag, a red field with the regional arms, was adopted in 2007.

features.role: Arms of Grodno Region - a golden aurochs beneath a mural crown
